title: Xbox lays off 60-70 Obsidian devs, guts Id Software coding team
body:
In a shocking turn of events, Microsoft's recent round of layoffs has left the gaming community reeling, with two of its most prominent studios, Id Software and Obsidian, bearing the brunt of the cuts. According to reports, the tech giant's decision to downsize its workforce has resulted in the loss of nearly all of Id Software's coding team, as well as a significant chunk of Obsidian's development staff, totaling around 60-70 employees. This drastic reduction in personnel has sent shockwaves throughout the gaming industry, leaving many to wonder about the future of these beloved studios and the potential impact on their upcoming projects.

The layoffs at Id Software, the legendary studio behind the Doom and Quake franchises, are particularly alarming, as they reportedly involve the majority of the company's coding team. This raises concerns about the studio's ability to continue developing and supporting its existing titles, let alone work on new projects. Id Software has been a cornerstone of the gaming industry for decades, and the loss of its experienced coders could have far-reaching consequences for the quality and stability of its games. The fact that Microsoft, a company with deep pockets and a strong commitment to the gaming sector, is willing to make such drastic cuts to one of its most iconic studios has left many in the industry scratching their heads.

The situation at Obsidian, the studio behind critically acclaimed titles like Pillars of Eternity and The Outer Worlds, is equally dire. The loss of 60-70 developers is a significant blow to the company, which has built a reputation for creating engaging, story-driven games. Obsidian has been a key player in Microsoft's gaming strategy, and the reduction in staff could jeopardize the studio's ability to deliver on its upcoming projects. The layoffs are also likely to have a profound impact on the morale of the remaining staff, who will be left to pick up the pieces and try to maintain the studio's high standards despite the significant reduction in personnel.

The scale of Microsoft's layoffs has sparked a wider debate about the company's commitment to the gaming industry. While Microsoft has been investing heavily in its gaming division, including the acquisition of several high-profile studios, the decision to cut so deeply into the workforce of two of its most prominent studios has raised questions about its long-term strategy. Some have speculated that the layoffs may be a sign of a broader shift in Microsoft's priorities, with the company potentially looking to focus more on its cloud gaming and subscription-based services. Others have pointed out that the layoffs may be a necessary evil, allowing Microsoft to streamline its operations and focus on its most promising projects.

As the news of the layoffs continues to sink in, the gaming community is left to wonder about the future of Id Software and Obsidian. Both studios have a rich history and a loyal fan base, and the prospect of seeing them diminished or dismantled is a bleak one. While Microsoft has yet to comment on the specifics of the layoffs, it is clear that the company is undergoing a significant transformation. As the gaming industry continues to evolve, it remains to be seen how Microsoft will balance its commitment to its studios with the need to adapt to changing market conditions.

As the dust settles on this latest round of layoffs, one thing is clear: the gaming industry is in a state of flux. The rise of cloud gaming, the growth of subscription-based services, and the increasing importance of online multiplayer have all created new challenges and opportunities for game developers. While the layoffs at Id Software and Obsidian are a setback for Microsoft's gaming division, they also present an opportunity for the company to regroup and refocus its efforts. As the industry looks to the future, it will be interesting to see how Microsoft navigates this challenging landscape and what the ultimate impact will be on its beloved studios and the games they create.
